design_philosophy.core_loop: Progress through side-scrolling stages by jumping on enemies, throwing apples, collecting gems, and avoiding obstacles. Bonus stages via scarab collection offer risk/reward mechanics for health and lives.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (health hearts and lives) balanced against obstacle avoidance and enemy encounters. Magic Carpet escape sequences introduce time pressure and high-speed obstacle navigation.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Experience Aladdin's adventure through Agrabah as depicted in the Disney film—platforming action grounded in film narrative with magical set pieces (Cave of Wonders escape, Magic Carpet ride).
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Designed by Shinji Mikami to prioritize animation quality, visual charm, and faithful film adaptation over challenge. Deliberately moderately-paced rather than Capcom's traditionally demanding titles. Mechanical distinction from Genesis version (no scimitar combat—apples-only approach).

difficulty.lives_system: Player starts with multiple extra lives. Lives lost when health depleted or falling off screen bottom. Game ends when lives exhausted.
difficulty.continues_system: Player can continue from current level as long as continues remain.
difficulty.checkpointing: Checkpoint-less progression per stage; death/continue resets to stage start.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Moderate. Bonus stages (scarab collection) provide optional risk/reward for extra lives and health upgrades. Health meter (starting 3 hearts) can be increased through pickups and bonuses, creating player-influenced resilience scaling.

progression.upgrade_system: Health heart pickups increase max/current health. Bonus stage wheel grants extra lives and special bonuses. Gem collection (especially ten red gems per stage) increases score and unlocks bonus content.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Scarab collection within stages unlocks bonus wheel stages. Linear stage unlock via completion (Stages 1-7 sequential). Hidden level select exists via controller 2 code input in options menu.
replayability_hooks: Gem collection for high scores and 10-red-gem thresholds per stage
Bonus stage wheel outcomes vary (multiple bonus types)
Magic Carpet bonus stage (between 5-6) ends when song ends—timing variable
Hidden level select for speedrun/challenge replays

world_building_techniques: Stage progression mirrors film narrative (Agrabah city exploration → Cave of Wonders escape → Magic Carpet ride with Jasmine)
Character cameos (Genie in bonus wheel, Jasmine in Magic Carpet stage)
Thematic level design tied to film locations

boss_design.philosophy: Story-narrative bosses tied to film events. Stages 1, 6, and 7 contain bosses; other stages emphasize obstacle/enemy gauntlets without climactic encounters.
notable_bosses: Stage 7 final boss (unspecified in sources—implied climactic confrontation)

danger_signals: Lava waves in Cave of Wonders requiring upward/downward traversal to escape
Falling hazards and screen-bottom death threshold
Enemy encounters requiring avoidance or jump-on dispatch
Magic Carpet collision obstacles in bonus stages
environmental_storytelling: Agrabah city stage design reflects Disney film geography
Cave of Wonders set piece with rising lava creates pressure narrative
Magic Carpet ride freely-flying stage visualizes romantic journey
Treasure chest → scarab item establishes reward iconography

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Disney's Aladdin (SNES)
